examples.title: Basic TCP Echo Server (Iterative)
examples.description: A simple iterative TCP echo server that handles one client at a time.
examples.explanation: This iterative server creates a listening socket, binds to port 8080, and accepts one client at a time. It echoes back any data received. Key functions: socket() creates the endpoint, bind() associates port, listen() enables connections, accept() gets client connection, recv()/send() exchange data, close() terminates.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) per client, where n is bytes transferred

examples.title: TCP Client Program
examples.description: A TCP client that connects to a server and sends data.
examples.explanation: This client demonstrates the client-side socket programming. Steps: socket() creates client socket, inet_pton() converts IP to binary, connect() establishes TCP connection (3-way handshake), send() transmits data, read() receives response, close() closes socket.

examples.title: Concurrent Fork-based TCP Server
examples.description: A concurrent TCP server that forks a child process to handle each client.
examples.explanation: This concurrent server handles multiple clients using fork(). Parent accepts connections and forks child for each client. Child handles communication and exits. Parent ignores SIGCHLD to prevent zombies. SO_REUSEADDR allows quick restart. Key: parent closes client_fd, child closes server_fd.
